key_insight: For shallow polynomial neural networks with affine targets, the loss landscape is fully characterized: global minima exist iff there are at least d active, visible hidden neurons with distinct pivots, while the cubic case admits a complete critical-point classification.
engineering_relevance: This gives a precise map of when training can succeed or fail, helping designers choose network width and detect parameter regimes where optimization will inevitably diverge or get trapped in non-global minima.
method: Analyze true-loss landscape for one-dimensional shallow PNNs with monomial activation.
Prove a sharp existence/non-existence criterion for global minimizers for arbitrary degree d.
Classify all critical points for cubic activation, including saddles and non-global local minima.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical results on the infimum, attainment, and critical-point structure of the loss, rather than empirical experiments or datasets.
watch_item: The results are specific to one-dimensional shallow PNNs with monomial activations and affine targets, so direct transfer to deeper networks or non-polynomial activations may not hold.
paper_title: Landscape analysis for shallow neural networks: Complete classification of critical points for cubic activation and affine target functions
